courses.title: Coronet Peak 2026
courses.summary: Queenstown's pioneer ski field — terrain, lessons, snowmaking and the 2026 capital programme. Pitch with confidence to all guest types.

modules.title: Mountain overview
modules.summary: Where Coronet Peak sits, who it suits, the headline 2026 changes.

blocks.text_md: **Coronet Peak** is NZSki's pioneer ski field — 25 minutes from Queenstown CBD, suited to all abilities with a strong intermediate and family draw. Open daily winter 2026 from **June 13** through to **early October**.
**New for 2026** — A South Island–first **Snow Factory** producing premium snow regardless of temperature, dramatically improving the early-season experience.
Headline 2026 capital programme: Kidzone carpet upgrade, new Snow Factory, **new Snowsports Heli Premium Private** experience launched alongside existing private-coaching tiers.

modules.title: Lifts & terrain map
modules.summary: Lift configuration, terrain mix, what to recommend for beginners vs advanced.

blocks.text_md: Lift network across Coronet's bowl plus the M1 Express on the back face. Beginner zone fed by Meadows and Kidzone carpets; intermediates spread across Greengates / Rocky Gully; experts head to Back Bowls when conditions allow.

modules.title: Lessons & coaching
modules.summary: Three private-coaching tiers — Premium, Performance, Premium Heli. Who they're for and how to position them.

blocks.text_md: Coronet Peak offers **three tiers of private coaching**, each capped at 4 guests per instructor. Choosing the right tier depends on ability level and what the guest wants to get out of the day.
**Premium Privates** — family-friendly, all abilities. **NZ$ from 1,099/day**. Recommend for first-timers through to confident intermediates and mixed-ability groups.
**Performance Privates** — technique-focused for advanced skiers/snowboarders. **NZ$ from 1,299/day**. Recommend when the guest's goal is measurable improvement.

**Premium Heli Privates** — backcountry heli access for advanced+ guests. **NZ$ from 2,890/day** including 2 drop-offs and full mountain-safety briefing. Subject to weather. Only available at Coronet Peak (not Remarkables or Mt Hutt).

modules.title: Pricing & inclusions
modules.summary: Lift passes, rentals, bundles, and trade rates.

blocks.text_md: Day lift passes start at **NZ$ 169 adult / 99 child** at gate; **advance purchase** discounts available 14+ days out. 3-mountain pass (Coronet + Remarkables + Mt Hutt) is the best value for guests staying 4+ days in NZ.

modules.title: Booking & FAQ
modules.summary: How agents book, snow-day policy, transport from Queenstown.

blocks.text_md: Trade bookings via the NZSki Travel Trade portal. **Snow-day cancellation policy:** if the resort closes for the full day, lift-pass holders receive a credit (not refund) usable within 24 months at any NZSki resort. Lessons and heli are rebooked subject to availability or fully refunded.

_sources: Products/NZSki/2026 Media Kit refresh - Coronet Peak.pdf

courses.title: The Remarkables 2026
courses.summary: Queenstown's family-favourite alpine resort, ringed by the iconic Remarkables range. 30% beginner, 40% intermediate terrain — pitch with confidence to families and intermediate-led groups.

modules.title: Mountain overview
modules.summary: Where The Remarkables sits, who it suits, the headline 2026 changes.

blocks.text_md: **The Remarkables** sits 24 km (45 min) from central Queenstown via 9 km sealed + 4 km unsealed access road. Targeted opening **13 June 2026**. Peak elevation **1,943 m**, vertical drop **468 m**, average snowfall **3.67 m per annum** (excl. snowmaking).
**New for 2026** — expanded café with 200+ additional seats, café-deck fire pits with BBQ + music, increased Ski Bus capacity (3 new MAN 4WD coaches + a 4th mid-season), and additional snow machines.

The Remarkables ski area is part of family-owned NZSki, alongside Coronet Peak (Queenstown) and Mt Hutt (Methven). Strong family draw plus enough advanced terrain (Alta Chutes / Homeward Bound) to satisfy expert guests on the same day.

modules.title: Terrain mix & lifts
modules.summary: 30/40/30 beginner-intermediate-advanced split. Lift network, named runs, where to send each guest type.

blocks.text_md: Terrain split: **30% beginner, 40% intermediate, 30% advanced**. Lifts: **three high-speed six-seater chairs**, one quad chair, and **four surface conveyors** (three with covered galleries).
**Iconic named runs**: City, Curvey, Cross Fall, Serpentine, Turquoise, Waterfall, Calypso, Mid Steeps — plus dedicated terrain parks.

**Where to send guests**: First-timers → Meadows / surface conveyors. Confident intermediates → Sugar Bowl + Shadow Basin. Advanced → Alta Chutes, Homeward Bound, and back-bowl access via Curvey traverses.

modules.title: Lessons, kids programmes & non-skiers
modules.summary: Kids programmes, private + group coaching, adaptive snowsports, and what non-skier guests can do.

blocks.text_md: Private and group coaching, kids programmes from age 3, high-performance camps, and adaptive snowsports. Capped class sizes mean confident progression for first-timers in a single day.
**Non-skier guests**: scenic chair rides on First Chair, lunch at the Aperol Ice Bar or main restaurant, sledding zone, and beginner snow-experience packages.

modules.title: Food, beverage & facilities
modules.summary: Restaurant, café, Aperol Ice Bar, Barista Bar — plus the 2026 deck expansion.

blocks.text_md: F&B lineup: full-service restaurant + bar, café, **Aperol Ice Bar**, and Barista Bar. The 2026 café expansion adds 200+ seats and a deck with fire pits, BBQ and a custom park sound system.

**Sustainability**: in 2024 NZSki introduced Sorting Stations across all outlets — reduced waste to landfill by 42% through composting. Carbon credits purchased via Climate Action. Ongoing planting, revegetation and pest control on the mountain.

modules.title: Booking, transport & FAQ
modules.summary: Ski Bus, trade portal, snow-day policy, multi-mountain pass economics.

blocks.text_md: **Ski Bus**: dedicated scheduled service from central Queenstown before 9 am and Frankton after 9.30 am. 2026 fleet expansion adds 3 new MAN 4WD coaches (+ 1 mid-season) — capacity issues common in 2024/25 are addressed.

**Snow-day policy** (NZSki-wide): if the resort closes for the full day, lift-pass holders receive a **credit** (not refund) usable within 24 months at any NZSki resort. Lessons/heli rebooked subject to availability or fully refunded.
**3-Mountain pass** (Coronet + Remarkables + Mt Hutt) is best value for guests staying **4+ days** in NZ. Strongly recommend for South Island multi-stop itineraries.

_sources: Products/NZSki/2026 Media Kit refresh - The Remarkables.pdf

courses.title: Mt Hutt 2026
courses.summary: South Island's highest commercial ski area and NZ's longest season. Methven-based, accessible from Christchurch — great for shoulder-season pitches and guests prioritising snow reliability.

modules.title: Mountain overview
modules.summary: Why Mt Hutt wins on snow reliability and shoulder season.

blocks.text_md: **Mt Hutt** is NZ's longest ski season — targeted opening **12 June 2026**, often running into mid-October. Peak elevation **2,086 m** (the highest commercial ski area in the South Island), vertical drop **683 m**. 1 hr 30 min drive from Christchurch International Airport via Methven.
**Positioning tip**: Mt Hutt is the answer when a guest wants to ski in **June** (Queenstown fields often patchy in early June) or in **October** when other fields have closed.
Sister to Coronet Peak and The Remarkables — same NZSki ownership, same lift-pass and booking systems. 3-Mountain pass holders enjoy all three on one ticket.

modules.title: Terrain, lifts & runs
modules.summary: Skiable area, lift configuration, the named runs and back-bowl access.

blocks.text_md: Mt Hutt offers wide-open faces, well-suited to intermediates building confidence. The high altitude means lighter, drier snow than Queenstown fields on most days.
**Lift network**: high-speed six-pack chair (Heritage Chair) + Summit Six + Triple chair + magic-carpet learner zone. The Six-Star Express opens up the South Face and back bowls in good weather.

modules.title: Lessons & coaching
modules.summary: Two private-coaching tiers (Premium, Performance). No heli at Mt Hutt — that's Coronet only.

blocks.text_md: Mt Hutt offers **two tiers of private coaching**: Premium Privates (family-friendly, all abilities, **NZ$ from 1,099/day**) and Performance Privates (advanced, technique-focused, **NZ$ from 1,299/day**). Each session is capped at 4 guests per instructor.
**Important**: Premium Heli Privates are **Coronet Peak only**. Don't promise heli access to guests buying a Mt Hutt-only itinerary — direct them to Coronet for the day if it's the differentiator.
Kids programmes from age 3, high-performance camps, and adaptive snowsports — same structure as the other two NZSki resorts.

modules.title: Events & what's new for 2026
modules.summary: Chillfest, Moon Ski, and the high-altitude festival programme.

blocks.text_md: **Chillfest** (19 Sep) — Mt Hutt's biggest party of the season, on-mountain music + après. **Moon Ski** (26 Sep) — limited-capacity night-skiing event under a full moon. Use these as 'reason to stay extra nights in Methven' when pitching trips.
Mid-week junior race series runs **27–28 Jun, 27–28 Jul, 28 Aug** — useful for parents wanting structured kid programming.

modules.title: Access, accommodation & booking
modules.summary: Methven base, Christchurch arrival logistics, where guests stay.

blocks.text_md: **Base town**: Methven (population ~1,800) — the entire town is geared around the ski season. Most guests stay 2–4 nights. Christchurch (1 hr 30 min drive) is the alternative for guests who want city amenities + day-trip skiing.
**Trade booking**: NZSki Travel Trade portal handles all three resorts in one PNR. Snow-day credit policy is identical to the other resorts (credit, not refund, valid 24 months at any NZSki resort).

_sources: Products/NZSki/2026 Media Kit refresh - Mt Hutt.pdf
